question.content: Determine the maximum recombination fraction between any two loci using the linked, independent-assortment, and unlinked scenarios in the figure. Output exactly one expression token (for example 1/3), plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a simplest fraction such as a/b; write only the fraction itself, with no explanatory text, units, or equals sign.

step_1.content: For any pair of loci, recombination fraction is bounded above by one half.
step_1.key_point: Recombination fraction for any loci pair is at most 1/2.

step_3.content: Independent assortment gives the limiting case where recombinant and parental classes approach parity.
step_3.key_point: This corresponds to the ceiling value.

step_5.content: Loci on different chromosomes or far apart on the same chromosome can both appear unlinked.
step_5.key_point: Multiple crossovers can mask linkage and make data look independent.

step_7.content: Therefore the maximal recombination frequency over all scenarios is one half.
step_7.key_point: Tightly linked values are below the same bound.
